instruction:
Let $f(x)$ be absolutely continuous on any interval $\left[ a, b \right] \subset \mathbf{R}^{1}$. Then, for each $y \in \mathbf{R}^{1}$, find the value of $\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} y} \!\int_{a}^{b} \! f(x+y) \mathrm{d} x$.

Options:
A. $$
\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} y} \int_{a}^{b} x f ( y ) \mathrm{d} x
$$
B. $$
\int_{a}^{b} {f ( x+y ) \cdot y} \mathrm{d} x
$$
C. $$
\int_{a}^{b} {\frac{\mathrm{d}} {\mathrm{d} x}} f ( x+y ) \mathrm{d} x
$$
D. $$
x \cdot \int_{a}^{b} {y f(x)} \mathrm{d} x
$$
E. $$
\int_{a}^{b} y \cdot {\frac{\mathrm{d}} {\mathrm{d} x}} f ( x ) \mathrm{d} x
$$
F. $$
\int_{a}^{b} {\frac{\mathrm{d}} {\mathrm{d} y}} f ( x+y ) \mathrm{d} x
$$
G. $$
f ( a+y ) \cdot \int_{a}^{b} 1 \mathrm{d} x
$$
H. $$
y \cdot \int_{a}^{b} {f(x)} \mathrm{d} x
$$
I. $$
\int_{a}^{b} {f ( x+y )} \mathrm{d} x
$$
J. $$
\int_{a}^{b} {f' ( x+y )} \mathrm{d} x
$$
